topic: The Incredible Heat of The Sun's Corona
title: Why the Sun's Outer Atmosphere Is So Much Hotter Than Its Surface

lesson.learn: The corona is the Sun's outer atmosphere, and it is astonishingly hot, reaching millions of degrees. That seems backward because the visible surface of the Sun is much cooler. The leading explanation is that magnetic energy is constantly being transported upward and released in the corona through reconnection, waves, and other heating processes.
lesson.simpler: The Sun's outer atmosphere is hotter than its surface because magnetic energy is being dumped there all the time.
lesson.keyTakeaway: The corona stays extremely hot because the Sun's magnetic field keeps injecting energy into it faster than it can cool down.

learnImageCredit.creator: Images courtesy of the STEREO Science Center

lesson.deeperDive: Scientists still debate exactly how the corona is heated so efficiently, but magnetic reconnection is a major piece of the puzzle. The corona is threaded with magnetic loops that twist, snap, and reconnect, dumping energy into the plasma. That same magnetic environment helps drive the solar wind and powers coronal mass ejections. Studying the corona matters because it is the starting point for much of the Sun-Earth space-weather chain.
lesson.funFact: The corona becomes easiest to study during a total solar eclipse, when the glare of the photosphere is temporarily blocked.
references: Solar corona and coronal heating review articles

people: Eugene Parker - proposed a hot solar corona
